capacity_evidence.device: one Blackhole p300c in a 1x1 TTNN mesh
capacity_evidence.failure_kind: TT_FATAL DRAM allocator out of memory
capacity_evidence.explanation: Serialized full translated-layer probes at the preserved batch of 32 passed through device-to-host output materialization at sequence 6528 and failed at the next tile-aligned length, 6560, when the MLP multiply requested a 9.01 GiB bf16 output buffer. At the HF limit, one [batch,32768,23040] bf16 MLP buffer is 45 GiB and gate, up, and product buffers alone require 135 GiB before residuals, weights, caches, and allocator overhead.

notes: This is the IR-translated functional starting point, not a reduced Falcon3 architecture limit. The IR emitted length-128 persistent caches; the translation parameterizes them and defaults to the measured 6528-token single-device boundary. Numerical PCC validation covers prefill through 128 and decode at position 17, while 6528 is a full translated-layer execution/capacity validation. Later optimized or chunked stages may extend context without materializing the dense functional-stage intermediates used here.
